snippets.title: ColumnTransformer with one-hot and ordinal branches
snippets.code:
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, OrdinalEncoder

preprocess = ColumnTransformer([
    ("plan_ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore"), ["plan"]),
    (
        "risk_ord",
        OrdinalEncoder(categories=[["low", "medium", "high"]]),
        ["risk_band"],
    ),
])

X_train_enc = preprocess.fit_transform(X_train)
X_val_enc = preprocess.transform(X_val)
snippets.explanation: Each branch applies a different encoder to named columns. Fit on train only; validation uses transform with frozen categories and dummy columns.

snippets.title: Full Pipeline for reproducible serving
snippets.code:
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
import joblib

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocess),
    ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000)),
])

pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
joblib.dump(pipe, "churn_model.joblib")

loaded = joblib.load("churn_model.joblib")
loaded.predict(X_production)
snippets.explanation: Serializing the entire pipeline ensures production applies the same encodings learned during training—critical for one-hot vocabularies and ordinal maps.

snippets.title: Frequency encoding computed on train only
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

freq_map = X_train["publisher_id"].value_counts(normalize=True)

X_train = X_train.copy()
X_train["publisher_freq"] = X_train["publisher_id"].map(freq_map).fillna(0.0)

X_test = X_test.copy()
X_test["publisher_freq"] = X_test["publisher_id"].map(freq_map).fillna(0.0)
snippets.explanation: Frequency statistics come from training counts only; unseen test publishers get 0.0 (or another documented fallback)—not a refit on test data.

snippets.title: Rare-level bucketing before encoding
snippets.code:
TOP_K = 500
top_merchants = X_train["merchant_id"].value_counts().head(TOP_K).index

def bucket_merchant(s):
    return s if s in top_merchants else "__other__"

X_train["merchant_bucket"] = X_train["merchant_id"].map(bucket_merchant)
X_test["merchant_bucket"] = X_test["merchant_id"].map(bucket_merchant)
snippets.explanation: Top-K bucketing reduces cardinality so one-hot or target encoding stays stable; the bucket list is derived from training frequency only.
